system.name: Honey Heist
system.description: You have a complex plan that requires precise timing. You are also a goddamn bear.
system.credits: A game by Grant Howitt (2017). With thanks to All Out of Bubblegum, 3:16, and (weirdly) Polaris.

stats.name: Bear
stats.description: Use to maul stuff, run and climb, shrug off damage, scare people, and generally do bear stuff.

stats.name: Criminal
stats.description: Use to do anything not directly related to being a bear.

mechanic.default: When you act and the outcome is in doubt, roll a d6. Equal to or under the relevant stat = success. Over = failure.
mechanic.advantage: If you are using your bear special skill or doing something related to your role, roll 2d6 and pick the lowest.

frustration.trigger: The plan fails and you run into difficulty.
frustration.effect: Move one point from Criminal into Bear.

creed.trigger: The plan goes off without a hitch.
creed.effect: Move one point from Bear into Criminal.

voluntaryShifts.method: Flashback scene where the bears plan the heist over coffee and cigarettes in the back room of a seedy bar.

criminalMaxed.result: Lured into a life of crime. You betray the party.

bearMaxed.result: You flip out bear-style and lose it. Animal control picks you up in about half an hour.

characterCreation._comment: Roll 3D6 to determine descriptor, role, and bear type. Audience does not build characters in the Mad Libs format; cast plays pre-built bears.

bearType.name: Panda
bearType.skill: Eat anything that looks like bamboo

bonusHat._comment: Roll 1d8 if your bear wants a hat.
options: Trilby
Top
Bowler
Flat-Cap
Cowboy
Fez
Crown
Roll Twice

gmTables._comment: Original Howitt setup tables, kept full as a GM fallback. The Mad Libs in showConfig override these with TMP-themed paths.
thePrize: Ultradense megahoney from especially posh bees
A briefcase of pure manuka extract worth $5m
The Queen of All Bees, once exiled, now returned
Black Orchid Honey, which turns anyone who eats it into a goth
Abraham Lincoln's beehive, thought to be haunted by his ghost
Miss Universe 2017, an especially attractive Bee
twist: This place is rigged to blow
There's a rival team of bears

options: Armed Guards
Electronically locked doors
Laser tripwire grids
CCTV network
"Impenetrable" Vault
Poison Gas

showConfig._comment: Mad Libs Honey Heist at Lucky Straws, May 23 2026. Four themed worlds: Theme Park, Outer Space, Time Travel, Movie Monster. Every Mad Lib has 4 options in that same order, so option index = theme. Audience can mix worlds freely; the cast plays whatever combo lands.

interactionMode.preShow: Audience reservations include a 5-question Setup Mad Lib that locks at showtime. Most-voted option wins per field.
interactionMode.live: Three smaller Mad Libs pushed in real time at narrative cues. Open 60 seconds each.

madLibs.title: The Setup
madLibs.prompt: Help us set the heist up before the bears even know what they're stealing. Pick the world. Pick the chaos. The cast plays whatever you choose.

fields.label: HoneyCon 2026 is being held at...
options: BeeWorld, a struggling theme park that just bet everything on a honey-themed rebrand
The Apiary, an orbital station whose artificial gravity is held together with rubber bands
A traveling festival that exists in 1976, 2014, and next Tuesday simultaneously
An abandoned soundstage where a cursed honey commercial was filmed in 1987

fields.label: The convention organizer is...
options: A relentlessly upbeat CEO who insists everyone call him Uncle Buzzy
A space-station admin who is three months past her psych eval
A historian from 2387 who claims she's already won this heist
A reclusive horror director who hasn't been seen in thirty years

fields.label: Aside from loads of honey, the real prize is...
options: The original animatronic Bee mascot, said to still hum
A jar of honey from the asteroid belt that pours sideways
A teaspoon of prehistoric honey, scooped from the very first hive
The final unreleased reel of Sting II: The Reckoning

fields.label: But little do the bears know...
options: The park mascots are real, sentient, and unionizing tonight
The station is gradually de-orbiting and nobody told the guests
They've already done this heist three times. They lost every time.
The thing they thought was a prop isn't a prop

fields.label: Also, somehow, also at HoneyCon...
options: A high school marching band that booked the same venue
A diplomatic delegation of sentient bees that just docked
The bears' own younger selves, who do not recognize them
A live YouTube ghost-hunting crew, filming everything

madLibs.title: The Disguise
madLibs.triggerCue: When the bears need to bluff past the front desk.
madLibs.prompt: What's the worst (best?) disguise these bears could possibly wear?

fields.label: The bears walk in dressed as...
options: The cast of the live mascot show, Ursine Spectacular!
Ambassadors from the Honeyjamm Cluster
Time-traveling beekeeping inspectors from a very specific year
The documentary crew that's been filming the whole convention

madLibs.title: The Complication
madLibs.triggerCue: Halfway through the heist, when something has to go wrong.
madLibs.prompt: Time for chaos. Pick the complication.

fields.label: Suddenly...
options: A roller coaster gets stuck mid-loop with families on board
Gravity blinks off for fifteen seconds. Then it blinks back on.
A second team of bears walks in. They are also from the future.
The 'prop' creature in the display case starts to move

madLibs.title: The Getaway
madLibs.triggerCue: Climax. The bears need to vanish.
madLibs.prompt: How do these bears get out alive?

fields.label: The escape vehicle is...
options: A commandeered parade float
An escape pod that turns out to be a confetti cannon
They jump back twenty minutes and try a different door
Through the soundstage door, straight into a different movie
